tests.name: Appearance
tests.limit: A pale cream or cream coloured powder.
tests.details: Observe the color and physical form of the substance.

tests.name: Solubility
tests.limit: Very slightly soluble in water; freely soluble in acetone; soluble in ethanol (96%); slightly soluble in chloroform.
tests.details: Assess solubility in water, acetone, ethanol (96%), and chloroform.

tests.name: Identification A (Infrared Absorption)
tests.limit: Concordant with the reference spectrum of oxyclozanide (RSV 33).
tests.details: Record the infrared absorption spectrum of the substance and compare it with the provided reference spectrum (RSV 33) as per Appendix II A.

tests.name: Identification B (UV Light Absorption)
tests.limit: A maximum only at 300 nm. The absorbance at the maximum is about 0.76.
tests.details: Prepare a 0.003% w/v solution in acidified methanol. Record the light absorption spectrum in the range 250 to 350 nm as per Appendix II B. Observe for a single maximum at 300 nm with an approximate absorbance of 0.76.

tests.name: Identification C (Melting Point)
tests.details: Determine the melting point of the substance according to Appendix V A.

tests.name: Ionisable Chlorine
tests.limit: Not more than 1.4 mL of 0.1M silver nitrate VS is required (0.25%).
tests.details: Dissolve 2 g of Oxyclozanide in 100 mL of methanol, add 10 mL of 1.5M nitric acid. Titrate with 0.1M silver nitrate VS, determining the endpoint potentiometrically.

tests.name: Related Substances
tests.limit: The area of any secondary peak with a retention time less than that of the principal peak is not greater than one third of the area of the principal peak in the chromatogram obtained with solution (2) (0.3%). The area of any secondary peak with a retention time greater than that of the principal peak is not greater than the area of the principal peak in the chromatogram obtained with solution (2) (1%).
tests.details: Carry out the method for liquid chromatography as per Appendix III D. Prepare solution (1) at 0.1% w/v in methanol/water with 0.1% v/v orthophosphoric acid (ratio similar to mobile phase). Prepare solution (2) by diluting 1 volume of solution (1) to 100 volumes with the mobile phase. Chromatographic conditions: Stainless steel column (20 cm × 4.6 mm) packed with octadecylsilyl silica gel for chromatography (5 µm, e.g., Hypersil ODS). Isocratic elution. Mobile phase: A mixture of methanol and water containing 0.1% v/v of orthophosphoric acid (typically 38 volumes of water and 62 volumes of methanol). Flow rate: 2 mL/minute. Ambient column temperature. Detection wavelength: 300 nm. Inject 20 µL of each solution.

tests.name: Loss on Drying
tests.limit: Not more than 1.0% of its weight.
tests.details: Dry 1 g of the substance to constant weight at 60°C at a pressure not exceeding 0.7 kPa.

tests.name: Sulfated Ash
tests.details: Perform the sulfated ash test according to Appendix IX A.

tests.name: Assay
tests.method: Non-aqueous potentiometric titration (Appendix VIII A, Method II)
tests.limit: Not less than 98.0% and not more than 101.0% of C13H6Cl5NO3, calculated with reference to the dried substance.
tests.details: Dissolve 0.25 g of the substance in 75 mL of anhydrous pyridine. Pass a current of nitrogen through the solution for 5 minutes and maintain it throughout the titration. Carry out Method II for non-aqueous titration (Appendix VIII A) using 0.1M tetrabutylammonium hydroxide VS as titrant and determining the end point potentiometrically. Each mL of 0.1M tetrabutylammonium hydroxide VS is equivalent to 20.07 mg of C13H6Cl5NO3.
